steps.title: Find the missing variable
steps.content: Pell et al. wanted to know whether perceived category similarity predicts brand-extension purchases. They had already calculated purchase-probability changes, so the missing data are ratings of perceived similarity between the product categories.

steps.title: Correlate similarity and buying
steps.content: The matching design collects category-similarity ratings for each extended-brand pair and compares them with the calculated probability changes. Choice B rates similarity within a category, while Choice C and Choice D focus on brand recognition rather than perceived category similarity. The answer is A.
